title: TreeChainComplex
summary: Packages first-kernel and Euler-characteristic targets for the prospective rank-general recurrence complex.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages first-kernel and Euler-characteristic targets for the prospective rank-general recurrence complex.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/TreeChainComplex.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Number of degree-s tree indices for a left-comb rank-r word, as a stars-and-bars expression. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Nat.choose s plus 2 times r minus 2 2 times r minus 2.

The expected generic dimension of ker d_1. For r >= 2, this is the closed form choose (k + r - 2) (r - 2). In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Nat.choose k plus r minus 2 r minus 2.

Euler characteristic of the expected rank-general recurrence complex. This is sum_q (-1)^q choose r q |I_{k-q}^T|. The subtraction in k - q is Lean's truncated natural subtraction; this is harmless for the first scaffold and can later be replaced by a dependent range over q <= min r k. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Finset.range r plus 1.sum the function sending q to -1 : the integers to the power q times Nat.choose r q : the integers times comb Index Card r k minus q : the integers.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{recurrenceEulerCharacteristic}(r,k)=(\operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{range} (r + 1)).\operatorname{sum} q \longmapsto ((-1 \colon \mathbb{Z}) ^ q) \cdot (\mathbb{N}.\operatorname{choose} r q \colon \mathbb{Z}) \cdot (\operatorname{combIndexCard} r (k - q) \colon \mathbb{Z})

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Statement that the Euler characteristic gives the expected generic dimension. This is the combinatorial target of the eventual generic exactness proof. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is recurrence Euler Characteristic r k equals generic Kernel Dimension Expected r k : the integers.

The first differential of the recurrence complex. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and a of type comb coeff space r s plus 1, its defining expression is d One n w A.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev firstDifferential {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : Fin r → CombCoeffSpace r s

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Membership in the kernel of the first differential. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and a of type comb coeff space r s plus 1, its defining expression is first Differential n w A equals fun j maps to fun β maps to 0.
correspondence.leanType: def InFirstKernel {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type Fin r maps to the real numbers, and A of type Comb Coeff Space r s plus 1, Rank Recurrence At Level n w A if and only if In First Kernel n w A.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,s\colon \mathbb{N},\;n\colon \mathbb{R},\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;A\colon \operatorname{CombCoeffSpace} r (s + 1),\quad \operatorname{RankRecurrenceAtLevel} n w A \Longleftrightarrow \operatorname{InFirstKernel} n w A
correspondence.leanType: theorem recurrence_iff_in_first_kernel {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : RankRecurrenceAtLevel n w A ↔ InFirstKernel n w A

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Package for a future proof of generic exactness. Its mathematical data are: euler formula: Generic Dimension Formula r k.

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: This file establishes syntax, aggregate imports, or an abstract bridge. It must not be read as a derivation of the corresponding geometric theorem from concrete tensor and analytic data.
